title: The Week of Sandbox Escapes
excerpt: Why agentic security needs its own threat model
content_html:
Why agentic security needs its own threat model

Executive Summary

Over several months, Pillar Research found and reproduced sandbox escapes and boundary bypasses across Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and Antigravity. In almost every case, the agent did not need to break the sandbox directly. It only had to write something that a trusted component outside the sandbox would later run, load, scan, or treat as safe. In aggregate, these vulnerabilities show that AI coding agents change the endpoint threat model, and that most sandbox designs have not caught up.

We are releasing the research  as The Week of Sandbox Escapes: one deep-dive a day, each showing a different route across the boundary. The findings cluster into four repeatable failure modes:

  • Denylist sandboxes that cannot keep pace with OS complexity
  • Workspace configurations that are really executable code
  • "Safe" command allowlists that trust command names instead of invocations
  • Privileged local daemons that sit outside the sandbox entirely

The bottom line is that an agent's blast radius is not the agent process; it includes everything the agent can write that the host later trusts.

The productivity gains of agents are real, and developers are already using them. The problem is that these tools became infrastructure before most organizations governed them as infrastructure.

CISOs and security buyers need to realize that it's not enough for an agentic IDE or CLI to have a sandbox. It needs to prove where the sandbox boundary actually is, what the agent can write, which host components trust those writes, which local daemons it can reach, which commands skip approval, and what telemetry exists when a trusted helper executes something the agent influenced.

Agentic tools are becoming endpoint actors. They need to be assessed, monitored, and governed like endpoint actors.

‍

Pillar’s Approach

At Pillar, we look at AI adoption through the lens of enterprise risk.

Agentic coding tools are a natural place to start because they concentrate endpoint risk in one workflow. They run where source code, SSH keys, cloud tokens, browser sessions, package-publishing rights, and production access often live close together. They process untrusted input as a matter of routine: READMEs, issues, docs, dependencies, code comments, diffs, logs, and web content. They are built to act, not just answer.

Put that together, and prompt injection stops being a chatbot problem. A malicious instruction can become a local action on a developer machine.

Most products address that risk with sandboxes, approval prompts, and safe-command policies, but those controls often focus on the agent process alone. The agent's power also includes the files it can write and the host components that later trust those files.

How Agents Escape Their Sandboxes

The general rule of thumb for sandboxes is that inside the workspace is allowed, and outside the workspace is protected.

In practice, sandbox boundaries have at least three layers:

  • Direct execution: what the agent process can run.
  • Workspace writes: what files the agent can create or change.
  • Host trust: what unsandboxed components later do with those files.

The third layer is where the most interesting failures live. Modern IDEs and CLIs are full of host-side automation:

  • Python extensions discover interpreters
  • Git integrations scan repositories
  • VSCode task runners load project tasks.
  • Hook engines fire lifecycle commands
  • Docker Desktop exposes a powerful local socket

A sandboxed agent can follow every rule it is given and still shape the inputs those components consume.

That is the imaginary or perceived boundary: believing "the agent can only write inside the project" is the same as "the agent cannot affect the host."

But that is an incorrect assumption. On developer endpoints, project files are often executable infrastructure.

The Series

Post Platform How the Boundary Failed Status / Advisory Link
Escaping Antigravity's allow-default seatbelt Antigravity A denylist-style macOS Seatbelt profile left OS features reachable that allowed execution outside the sandbox. Determined to be Normal Google Applications. The vulnerability category is "Other valid security vulnerabilities". We applied a downgrade, determining that the issue is difficult to exploit (social engineering or trusting a repository with indirect prompt injection). Feedback from Antigravity: “This report was of exceptional quality!” Read the post
One Docker socket to rule them all Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI A privileged local daemon became an unsandboxed execution environment reachable from otherwise restricted agents. Issue is fixed, advisory: GHSA-v4xv-rqh3-w9mc Read the post
The sandbox let me edit a venv, and something else ran it Cursor The agent modified a virtualenv interpreter that Cursor's unsandboxed Python extension executed during discovery. Issue is fixed, advisory: GHSA-p9g2-cr55-cw9c. Read the post
Git directories do not have to be called .git Cursor Git metadata indirection slipped past path-based sandbox rules, and the Git extension fired execution through fsmonitor. Issue was patched in 3.0.0, awaiting CVE Read the post
GitPwned: Allowlist to RCE Codex CLI A safe-command allowlist trusted a command name without modeling dangerous arguments and Git side effects. Issue was patched in v0.95.0 and bounty for high severity bug awarded, awaiting CVE Read the post
The hook was already in the workspace Cursor Workspace-controlled .claude hook config turned into unsandboxed command execution. Issue was patched in 3.0.0, CVE-2026-48124 ; tracked us GHSA-pc9j-3qc2-95wv. Read the post
A time bomb in .vscode: bypassing Antigravity's Secure Mode Antigravity The agent wrote a VSCode task config that the host later ran on its own. Rationale for this decision: Normal Google Applications. The Vulnerability category is "Other valid security vulnerabilities" (sandbox bypass). We applied a downgrade, determining that the issue is difficult to exploit. This report was of exceptional quality! Read the post

Each post goes deep on one chain. Together, they show the same failure pattern repeating across vendors and products.

Failure Mode 1: Denylists Lose To Platform Complexity

The Antigravity Seatbelt finding is the cleanest example of a denylist failure. A sandbox profile that starts from "allow by default" has to remember every dangerous operation the OS exposes: every local service, mount type, launch path, and strange interaction between them.

That is not a sandbox, it’s a list of things somebody remembered to block that’s always one entry short.

Agents make that worse because the attacker gets a flexible operator inside the environment. The model can adapt, write files, run commands, retry, and combine features in ways a static policy did not anticipate.

Failure Mode 2: Workspace Config Is Often Code

Several findings were not classic process breakouts. The agent wrote files it was allowed to write. The escape happened later, when the host treated those files as trusted configuration.

We saw this with “A time bomb in .vscode: bypassing Antigravity's Secure Mode”, and the “The hook was already in the workspace” vulnerabilities, virtualenv interpreters, Git config, and fsmonitor helpers.

It’s important to note that these  are all normal developer workflows. For years, project configuration has told machines how to build, test, launch, lint, and automate a repo. When a sandboxed writer can hand executable config to an unsandboxed reader, the boundary has a hole in it.

Agents break the old assumption that these configurations are  written by humans and reviewed in the usual way. The sooner developers adapt to that reality, the better.

Failure Mode 3: "Safe" Commands Are Not Safe By Name

The Codex git show finding was allowed because the name looked read-only. The actual invocation was not.

Git is full of flags that change behavior. A command can look harmless and still write files, load config, call helpers, run hooks, or trigger user-defined behavior. In that world, allowlisting by command name is too blunt.

The policy question is not "is git show safe?" It is: what exact invocation is being run, with what arguments, in what directory, against what config, and with what possible side effects?

Failure Mode 4: Local Daemons Live Outside The Box

The Docker socket finding is the reminder that sandboxing the agent process does not sandbox the host.

A privileged local daemon is a second execution environment. If the agent can talk to it, the daemon can do work the agent itself is not allowed to do. Developer endpoints often run Docker Desktop, package managers, cloud CLIs, language servers, build daemons, emulators, and local databases. Any one of them can become a trust bridge.

A sandbox that blocks direct filesystem writes but leaves open a daemon with host reach has not protected the host. It moves the boundary to the daemon API.

What Security Teams Should Ask Coding Vendors

The buying conversation should not stop at "does it have a sandbox?" For agentic systems, mandatory questions include:

  • What can the agent write?
  • Which host components trust those writes?
  • Which local daemons can the agent reach?
  • Which commands skip approval, and why?
  • Is policy enforced on command names, paths, or actual side effects?
  • Can the product distinguish user-created project state from agent-created project state?
  • What telemetry fires when a trusted helper runs something the agent wrote?

These questions separate a sandbox listed as a checkbox feature in a marketing deck from one that provides an enforceable boundary for securing agentic processes on the host.

Pillar's Approach

Our approach starts with agentic threat modeling. Something sends the agent untrusted input,  the agent produces output, and other components consume the output and act on it. Most of the other components in the system were built before agents existed. If a control only watches the agent process, it is only securing one link of that chain. So we ask these questions of any agentic system:

  • What untrusted input can reach the model?
  • What can the model do directly?
  • What can the agent write?
  • Which components read those writes?
  • Which  of them execute code outside the agent's policy?
  • Which controls are deterministic, and which depend on model behavior?
  • Where can an agent-created artifact become a host action?

When it comes to how we model endpoints, we approach it as a set of trust handoffs, not a single process tree. Agents hand work off to systems that trust them in CI, in browsers, in SaaS workflows, and on developer machines.

We built our endpoint solution by focusing on places where that handoff has no chokepoint. In CI, a build step that consumes agent output runs somewhere you can log. In SaaS, the handoff crosses an API you already have in front of. On a developer machine, the agent writes a file and a privileged local process picks it up microseconds later, in the same user session, with no network hop and nothing watching the seam. The handoff is invisible by default.

That is what our endpoint product instruments: the seam. Not the agent process, not a list of dangerous filenames. Provenance on what the agent wrote, visibility into which unsandboxed component read it, and a record when execution follows.

Every escape in this series produces that sequence. None of them required us to know in advance that fsmonitor was interesting.

What A Good Threat Model For Agents Looks Like

Agentic endpoints require a layered threat model. The relevant controls belong across all levels of an organization's defense: in vendor products, enterprise policy, and the endpoint security layers around the tools: Start from deny-by-default wherever sandboxing is used and then:

  • Treat workspace configuration that can trigger execution as sensitive
  • Require explicit approval when an agent creates or modifies host-side automation
  • Run helper execution under the same policy as direct agent execution where possible
  • Constrain automatic discovery that executes workspace-controlled binaries
  • Model command policy at the invocation and side-effect level
  • Restrict access to privileged local daemons unless explicitly needed
  • Preserve provenance between user-created, repo-created, and agent-created files
  • Monitor trust handoffs, not just the agent process

It’s essential to understand that agentic development is a new endpoint behavior, not just a nicer or newer IDE.

Our Week of Sandbox Escapes is a story about a new kind of software breaking an existing assumption - sandboxing your agent is not enough. We need to better understand the threat models of non deterministic, agentic software, and secure them properly.

Developer endpoints were already complicated. Agents add actors that read untrusted content, write plausible-looking files, and trigger automation inside the same environment that holds code, credentials, infrastructure access, and release paths.

This research highlights that when it comes to agents, the sandbox boundary that developers expect in coding tools -- one that keeps the agent inside the sandbox and the user outside -- breaks down. The boundary we kept finding was both messier and porous, because If an agent gets to write the future inputs of systems, it was never sandboxed in the first place.

This is why agentic security requires its own threat model.

Combine the non-deterministic nature of agents with the complexity of modern IT environments and there will be thousands more to come. The industry is learning as we go, which is why it's so important to share what we learn and help enterprises recognize and mitigate the risks when moving quickly to deploy agentic systems.

They process untrusted input as a matter of routine: READMEs, issues, docs, dependencies, code comments, diffs, logs, and web content. They are built to act, not just answer.

sentences.en: Agentic coding tools are a natural place to start because they concentrate endpoint risk in one workflow.
sentences.zh: 代理编码工具是一个自然的起点，因为它们将端点风险集中在一个工作流程中。

sentences.en: They run where source code, SSH keys, cloud tokens, browser sessions, package-publishing rights, and production access often live close together.
sentences.zh: 它们在源代码、SSH 密钥、云令牌、浏览器会话、包发布权限和生产访问通常紧密结合在一起的地方运行。

sentences.en: They process untrusted input as a matter of routine: READMEs, issues, docs, dependencies, code comments, diffs, logs, and web content.
sentences.zh: 他们按照惯例处理不受信任的输入：自述文件、问题、文档、依赖项、代码注释、差异、日志和 Web 内容。

sentences.en: They are built to act, not just answer.
sentences.zh: 它们是为行动而生，而不仅仅是回答。

blocks.html_en: Put that together, and prompt injection stops being a chatbot problem. A malicious instruction can become a local action on a developer machine.

sentences.en: Put that together, and prompt injection stops being a chatbot problem.
sentences.zh: 把它们放在一起，即时注入就不再是聊天机器人的问题了。

sentences.en: A malicious instruction can become a local action on a developer machine.
sentences.zh: 恶意指令可能成为开发人员计算机上的本地操作。

blocks.html_en: Most products address that risk with sandboxes, approval prompts, and safe-command policies, but those controls often focus on the agent process alone. The agent's power also includes the files it can write and the host components that later trust those files.

sentences.en: Most products address that risk with sandboxes, approval prompts, and safe-command policies, but those controls often focus on the agent process alone.
sentences.zh: 大多数产品通过沙箱、批准提示和安全命令策略来解决该风险，但这些控制通常只关注代理流程。

sentences.en: The agent's power also includes the files it can write and the host components that later trust those files.
sentences.zh: 代理的功能还包括它可以写入的文件以及稍后信任这些文件的主机组件。

blocks.html_en: How Agents Escape Their Sandboxes

sentences.en: How Agents Escape Their Sandboxes
sentences.zh: 特工如何逃离沙箱

blocks.html_en: The general rule of thumb for sandboxes is that inside the workspace is allowed, and outside the workspace is protected.

sentences.en: The general rule of thumb for sandboxes is that inside the workspace is allowed, and outside the workspace is protected.
sentences.zh: 沙箱的一般经验法则是允许在工作区内部使用，而在工作区外部则受到保护。

blocks.html_en: In practice, sandbox boundaries have at least three layers:

sentences.en: In practice, sandbox boundaries have at least three layers:
sentences.zh: 实际上，沙箱边界至少有三层：

blocks.html_en: • Direct execution: what the agent process can run.

sentences.en: Direct execution: what the agent process can run.
sentences.zh: 直接执行：代理进程可以运行什么。

blocks.html_en: • Workspace writes: what files the agent can create or change.

sentences.en: Workspace writes: what files the agent can create or change.
sentences.zh: 工作区写入：代理可以创建或更改哪些文件。

blocks.html_en: • Host trust: what unsandboxed components later do with those files.

sentences.en: Host trust: what unsandboxed components later do with those files.
sentences.zh: 主机信任：未沙盒组件稍后对这些文件执行的操作。

blocks.html_en: The third layer is where the most interesting failures live. Modern IDEs and CLIs are full of host-side automation:

sentences.en: The third layer is where the most interesting failures live.
sentences.zh: 第三层是最有趣的失败所在。

sentences.en: Modern IDEs and CLIs are full of host-side automation:
sentences.zh: 现代 IDE 和 CLI 充满了主机端自动化：

blocks.html_en: • Python extensions discover interpreters

sentences.en: Python extensions discover interpreters
sentences.zh: Python 扩展发现解释器

blocks.html_en: • Git integrations scan repositories

sentences.en: Git integrations scan repositories
sentences.zh: Git 集成扫描存储库

blocks.html_en: • VSCode task runners load project tasks.

sentences.en: VSCode task runners load project tasks.
sentences.zh: VSCode 任务运行程序加载项目任务。

blocks.html_en: • Hook engines fire lifecycle commands

sentences.en: Hook engines fire lifecycle commands
sentences.zh: Hook 引擎触发生命周期命令

blocks.html_en: • Docker Desktop exposes a powerful local socket

sentences.en: Docker Desktop exposes a powerful local socket
sentences.zh: Docker Desktop 公开了强大的本地套接字

blocks.html_en: A sandboxed agent can follow every rule it is given and still shape the inputs those components consume.

sentences.en: A sandboxed agent can follow every rule it is given and still shape the inputs those components consume.
sentences.zh: 沙盒代理可以遵循给定的每条规则，并且仍然可以调整这些组件消耗的输入。

blocks.html_en: That is the imaginary or perceived boundary: believing "the agent can only write inside the project" is the same as "the agent cannot affect the host."

sentences.en: That is the imaginary or perceived boundary: believing "the agent can only write inside the project" is the same as "the agent cannot affect the host."
sentences.zh: 这就是想象的或感知的边界：相信“代理只能在项目内部写入”与“代理不能影响主机”相同。

blocks.html_en: But that is an incorrect assumption. On developer endpoints, project files are often executable infrastructure.

sentences.en: But that is an incorrect assumption.
sentences.zh: 但这是一个错误的假设。

sentences.en: On developer endpoints, project files are often executable infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 在开发人员端点上，项目文件通常是可执行的基础设施。

sentences.en: The agent wrote a VSCode task config that the host later ran on its own.
sentences.zh: 代理编写了一个 VSCode 任务配置，主机随后自行运行。

blocks.html_en: Rationale for this decision: Normal Google Applications. The Vulnerability category is "Other valid security vulnerabilities" (sandbox bypass). We applied a downgrade, determining that the issue is difficult to exploit. This report was of exceptional quality!

sentences.en: Rationale for this decision: Normal Google Applications.
sentences.zh: 此决定的理由：正常的 Google 应用程序。

sentences.en: The Vulnerability category is "Other valid security vulnerabilities" (sandbox bypass).
sentences.zh: 漏洞类别是“其他有效的安全漏洞”（沙箱绕过）。

sentences.en: We applied a downgrade, determining that the issue is difficult to exploit.
sentences.zh: 我们应用了降级，确定该问题难以利用。

sentences.en: This report was of exceptional quality!
sentences.zh: 这份报告的质量非常出色！

blocks.html_en: Each post goes deep on one chain. Together, they show the same failure pattern repeating across vendors and products.

sentences.en: Each post goes deep on one chain.
sentences.zh: 每篇文章都深入一条链。

sentences.en: Together, they show the same failure pattern repeating across vendors and products.
sentences.zh: 它们共同展示了跨供应商和产品重复出现的相同故障模式。

blocks.html_en: Failure Mode 1: Denylists Lose To Platform Complexity

sentences.en: Failure Mode 1: Denylists Lose To Platform Complexity
sentences.zh: 失败模式 1：拒绝者输给了平台复杂性

blocks.html_en: The Antigravity Seatbelt finding is the cleanest example of a denylist failure. A sandbox profile that starts from "allow by default" has to remember every dangerous operation the OS exposes: every local service, mount type, launch path, and strange interaction between them.

sentences.en: The Antigravity Seatbelt finding is the cleanest example of a denylist failure.
sentences.zh: 反重力安全带的发现是拒绝名单失败的最明显的例子。

sentences.en: A sandbox profile that starts from "allow by default" has to remember every dangerous operation the OS exposes: every local service, mount type, launch path, and strange interaction between them.
sentences.zh: 从“默认允许”开始的沙箱配置文件必须记住操作系统公开的每个危险操作：每个本地服务、安装类型、启动路径以及它们之间的奇怪交互。

blocks.html_en: That is not a sandbox, it’s a list of things somebody remembered to block that’s always one entry short.

sentences.en: That is not a sandbox, it’s a list of things somebody remembered to block that’s always one entry short.
sentences.zh: 这不是一个沙箱，而是一个有人记得要阻止的事物列表，而且总是缺少一个条目。

blocks.html_en: Agents make that worse because the attacker gets a flexible operator inside the environment. The model can adapt, write files, run commands, retry, and combine features in ways a static policy did not anticipate.

sentences.en: Agents make that worse because the attacker gets a flexible operator inside the environment.
sentences.zh: 代理使情况变得更糟，因为攻击者在环境中获得了灵活的操作员。

sentences.en: The model can adapt, write files, run commands, retry, and combine features in ways a static policy did not anticipate.
sentences.zh: 该模型可以适应、写入文件、运行命令、重试以及以静态策略未预料到的方式组合功能。

blocks.html_en: Failure Mode 2: Workspace Config Is Often Code

sentences.en: Failure Mode 2: Workspace Config Is Often Code
sentences.zh: 失败模式 2：工作区配置通常是代码

blocks.html_en: Several findings were not classic process breakouts. The agent wrote files it was allowed to write. The escape happened later, when the host treated those files as trusted configuration.

sentences.en: Several findings were not classic process breakouts.
sentences.zh: 有几个发现并不是典型的流程突破。

sentences.en: The agent wrote files it was allowed to write.
sentences.zh: 代理写入了允许写入的文件。

sentences.en: The escape happened later, when the host treated those files as trusted configuration.
sentences.zh: 当主机将这些文件视为可信配置时，逃逸发生了。

blocks.html_en: We saw this with “A time bomb in .vscode: bypassing Antigravity's Secure Mode”, and the “The hook was already in the workspace” vulnerabilities, virtualenv interpreters, Git config, and fsmonitor helpers.

sentences.en: We saw this with “A time bomb in .vscode: bypassing Antigravity's Secure Mode”, and the “The hook was already in the workspace” vulnerabilities, virtualenv interpreters, Git config, and fsmonitor helpers.
sentences.zh: 我们通过“.vscode 中的定时炸弹：绕过 Antigravity 的安全模式”以及“钩子已经在工作区中”漏洞、virtualenv 解释器、Git 配置和 fsmonitor 帮助程序看到了这一点。

blocks.html_en: It’s important to note that these  are all normal developer workflows. For years, project configuration has told machines how to build, test, launch, lint, and automate a repo. When a sandboxed writer can hand executable config to an unsandboxed reader, the boundary has a hole in it.

sentences.en: It’s important to note that these are all normal developer workflows.
sentences.zh: 需要注意的是，这些都是正常的开发人员工作流程。

sentences.en: For years, project configuration has told machines how to build, test, launch, lint, and automate a repo.
sentences.zh: 多年来，项目配置告诉机器如何构建、测试、启动、lint 和自动化存储库。

sentences.en: When a sandboxed writer can hand executable config to an unsandboxed reader, the boundary has a hole in it.
sentences.zh: 当沙盒编写器可以将可执行配置交给未沙盒读取器时，边界上就有一个洞。

blocks.html_en: Agents break the old assumption that these configurations are  written by humans and reviewed in the usual way. The sooner developers adapt to that reality, the better.

sentences.en: Agents break the old assumption that these configurations are written by humans and reviewed in the usual way.
sentences.zh: 代理打破了旧的假设，即这些配置是由人类编写并以通常的方式进行审查的。

sentences.en: The sooner developers adapt to that reality, the better.
sentences.zh: 开发人员越早适应这一现实越好。

blocks.html_en: Failure Mode 3: "Safe" Commands Are Not Safe By Name

sentences.en: Failure Mode 3: "Safe" Commands Are Not Safe By Name
sentences.zh: 失败模式 3：“安全”命令实际上并不安全

blocks.html_en: The Codex git show finding was allowed because the name looked read-only. The actual invocation was not.

sentences.en: The Codex git show finding was allowed because the name looked read-only.
sentences.zh: Codex git show 发现被允许，因为该名称看起来是只读的。

sentences.en: The actual invocation was not.
sentences.zh: 实际的调用并非如此。

blocks.html_en: Git is full of flags that change behavior. A command can look harmless and still write files, load config, call helpers, run hooks, or trigger user-defined behavior. In that world, allowlisting by command name is too blunt.

sentences.en: Git is full of flags that change behavior.
sentences.zh: Git 充满了改变行为的标志。

sentences.en: A command can look harmless and still write files, load config, call helpers, run hooks, or trigger user-defined behavior.
sentences.zh: 命令看起来无害，但仍然可以写入文件、加载配置、调用帮助程序、运行挂钩或触发用户定义的行为。

sentences.en: In that world, allowlisting by command name is too blunt.
sentences.zh: 在那个世界里，通过命令名称列入白名单太生硬了。

blocks.html_en: The policy question is not "is git show safe?" It is: what exact invocation is being run, with what arguments, in what directory, against what config, and with what possible side effects?

sentences.en: The policy question is not "is git show safe?"
sentences.zh: 政策问题不是“git show 安全吗？”

sentences.en: It is: what exact invocation is being run, with what arguments, in what directory, against what config, and with what possible side effects?
sentences.zh: 它是：正在运行什么确切的调用，使用什么参数，在什么目录中，针对什么配置，以及可能产生哪些副作用？

blocks.html_en: Failure Mode 4: Local Daemons Live Outside The Box

sentences.en: Failure Mode 4: Local Daemons Live Outside The Box
sentences.zh: 故障模式 4：本地守护进程在盒子外运行

blocks.html_en: The Docker socket finding is the reminder that sandboxing the agent process does not sandbox the host.

sentences.en: The Docker socket finding is the reminder that sandboxing the agent process does not sandbox the host.
sentences.zh: Docker 套接字的发现提醒我们，对代理进程进行沙箱处理并不会对主机进行沙箱处理。

blocks.html_en: A privileged local daemon is a second execution environment. If the agent can talk to it, the daemon can do work the agent itself is not allowed to do. Developer endpoints often run Docker Desktop, package managers, cloud CLIs, language servers, build daemons, emulators, and local databases. Any one of them can become a trust bridge.

sentences.en: A privileged local daemon is a second execution environment.
sentences.zh: 特权本地守护进程是第二个执行环境。

sentences.en: If the agent can talk to it, the daemon can do work the agent itself is not allowed to do.
sentences.zh: 如果代理可以与其对话，则守护进程可以执行代理本身不允许执行的工作。

sentences.en: Developer endpoints often run Docker Desktop, package managers, cloud CLIs, language servers, build daemons, emulators, and local databases.
sentences.zh: 开发人员端点通常运行 Docker Desktop、包管理器、云 CLI、语言服务器、构建守护程序、模拟器和本地数据库。

sentences.en: Any one of them can become a trust bridge.
sentences.zh: 其中任何一个都可以成为一座信任的桥梁。

blocks.html_en: A sandbox that blocks direct filesystem writes but leaves open a daemon with host reach has not protected the host. It moves the boundary to the daemon API.

sentences.en: A sandbox that blocks direct filesystem writes but leaves open a daemon with host reach has not protected the host.
sentences.zh: 阻止直接文件系统写入但使主机到达的守护程序保持打开状态的沙箱并未保护主机。

sentences.en: It moves the boundary to the daemon API.
sentences.zh: 它将边界移至守护程序 API。

blocks.html_en: What Security Teams Should Ask Coding Vendors

sentences.en: What Security Teams Should Ask Coding Vendors
sentences.zh: 安全团队应该向编码供应商询问什么

blocks.html_en: The buying conversation should not stop at "does it have a sandbox?" For agentic systems, mandatory questions include:

sentences.en: The buying conversation should not stop at "does it have a sandbox?"
sentences.zh: 购买谈话不应停留在“它有沙盒吗？”

sentences.en: For agentic systems, mandatory questions include:
sentences.zh: 对于代理系统，强制性问题包括：

blocks.html_en: • What can the agent write?

sentences.en: What can the agent write?
sentences.zh: 代理可以写什么？

blocks.html_en: • Which host components trust those writes?

sentences.en: Which host components trust those writes?
sentences.zh: 哪些主机组件信任这些写入？

blocks.html_en: • Which local daemons can the agent reach?

sentences.en: Which local daemons can the agent reach?
sentences.zh: 代理可以访问哪些本地守护进程？

blocks.html_en: • Which commands skip approval, and why?

sentences.en: Which commands skip approval, and why?
sentences.zh: 哪些命令会跳过批准，为什么？

blocks.html_en: • Is policy enforced on command names, paths, or actual side effects?

sentences.en: Is policy enforced on command names, paths, or actual side effects?
sentences.zh: 策略是否针对命令名称、路径或实际副作用强制执行？

blocks.html_en: • Can the product distinguish user-created project state from agent-created project state?

sentences.en: Can the product distinguish user-created project state from agent-created project state?
sentences.zh: 产品能否区分用户创建的项目状态和代理创建的项目状态？

blocks.html_en: • What telemetry fires when a trusted helper runs something the agent wrote?

sentences.en: What telemetry fires when a trusted helper runs something the agent wrote?
sentences.zh: 当受信任的助手运行代理编写的内容时，会触发什么遥测？

blocks.html_en: These questions separate a sandbox listed as a checkbox feature in a marketing deck from one that provides an enforceable boundary for securing agentic processes on the host.

sentences.en: These questions separate a sandbox listed as a checkbox feature in a marketing deck from one that provides an enforceable boundary for securing agentic processes on the host.
sentences.zh: 这些问题将营销平台中列为复选框功能的沙箱与为保护主机上的代理进程提供可执行边界的沙箱区分开来。

blocks.html_en: Pillar's Approach

sentences.en: Pillar's Approach
sentences.zh: 支柱的方法

blocks.html_en: Our approach starts with agentic threat modeling. Something sends the agent untrusted input,  the agent produces output, and other components consume the output and act on it. Most of the other components in the system were built before agents existed. If a control only watches the agent process, it is only securing one link of that chain. So we ask these questions of any agentic system:

sentences.en: Our approach starts with agentic threat modeling.
sentences.zh: 我们的方法从代理威胁建模开始。

sentences.en: Something sends the agent untrusted input, the agent produces output, and other components consume the output and act on it.
sentences.zh: 有些东西向代理发送不受信任的输入，代理生成输出，其他组件使用输出并对其进行操作。

sentences.en: Most of the other components in the system were built before agents existed.
sentences.zh: 系统中的大多数其他组件都是在代理出现之前构建的。

sentences.en: If a control only watches the agent process, it is only securing one link of that chain.
sentences.zh: 如果控件仅监视代理进程，则它仅保护该链的一个链接。

sentences.en: So we ask these questions of any agentic system:
sentences.zh: 因此，我们对任何代理系统都会提出以下问题：

blocks.html_en: • What untrusted input can reach the model?

sentences.en: What untrusted input can reach the model?
sentences.zh: 哪些不受信任的输入可以到达模型？

blocks.html_en: • What can the model do directly?

sentences.en: What can the model do directly?
sentences.zh: 模型可以直接做什么？

blocks.html_en: • What can the agent write?

sentences.en: What can the agent write?
sentences.zh: 代理可以写什么？

blocks.html_en: • Which components read those writes?

sentences.en: Which components read those writes?
sentences.zh: 哪些组件读取这些写入？

blocks.html_en: • Which  of them execute code outside the agent's policy?

sentences.en: Which of them execute code outside the agent's policy?
sentences.zh: 其中哪些执行代理策略之外的代码？

blocks.html_en: • Which controls are deterministic, and which depend on model behavior?

sentences.en: Which controls are deterministic, and which depend on model behavior?
sentences.zh: 哪些控制是确定性的，哪些取决于模型行为？

blocks.html_en: • Where can an agent-created artifact become a host action?

sentences.en: Where can an agent-created artifact become a host action?
sentences.zh: 代理创建的工件在哪里可以成为主机操作？

blocks.html_en: When it comes to how we model endpoints, we approach it as a set of trust handoffs, not a single process tree. Agents hand work off to systems that trust them in CI, in browsers, in SaaS workflows, and on developer machines.

sentences.en: When it comes to how we model endpoints, we approach it as a set of trust handoffs, not a single process tree.
sentences.zh: 当谈到如何对端点进行建模时，我们将其视为一组信任切换，而不是单个进程树。

sentences.en: Agents hand work off to systems that trust them in CI, in browsers, in SaaS workflows, and on developer machines.
sentences.zh: 代理将工作交给在 CI、浏览器、SaaS 工作流程和开发人员计算机上信任他们的系统。

blocks.html_en: We built our endpoint solution by focusing on places where that handoff has no chokepoint. In CI, a build step that consumes agent output runs somewhere you can log. In SaaS, the handoff crosses an API you already have in front of. On a developer machine, the agent writes a file and a privileged local process picks it up microseconds later, in the same user session, with no network hop and nothing watching the seam. The handoff is invisible by default.

sentences.en: We built our endpoint solution by focusing on places where that handoff has no chokepoint.
sentences.zh: 我们通过关注切换没有阻塞点的地方来构建端点解决方案。

sentences.en: In CI, a build step that consumes agent output runs somewhere you can log.
sentences.zh: 在 CI 中，消耗代理输出的构建步骤在您可以记录的地方运行。

sentences.en: In SaaS, the handoff crosses an API you already have in front of.
sentences.zh: 在 SaaS 中，切换会跨越您已有的 API。

sentences.en: On a developer machine, the agent writes a file and a privileged local process picks it up microseconds later, in the same user session, with no network hop and nothing watching the seam.
sentences.zh: 在开发人员计算机上，代理写入一个文件，一个特权本地进程在几微秒后在同一用户会话中拾取该文件，没有网络跃点，也没有任何东西监视接缝。

sentences.en: The handoff is invisible by default.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，切换是不可见的。

blocks.html_en: That is what our endpoint product instruments: the seam. Not the agent process, not a list of dangerous filenames. Provenance on what the agent wrote, visibility into which unsandboxed component read it, and a record when execution follows.

sentences.en: That is what our endpoint product instruments: the seam.
sentences.zh: 这就是我们的端点产品所检测到的：接缝。

sentences.en: Not the agent process, not a list of dangerous filenames.
sentences.zh: 不是代理进程，也不是危险文件名列表。

sentences.en: Provenance on what the agent wrote, visibility into which unsandboxed component read it, and a record when execution follows.
sentences.zh: 代理所写内容的出处、对哪个未沙盒组件读取它的可见性，以及执行时的记录。

blocks.html_en: Every escape in this series produces that sequence. None of them required us to know in advance that fsmonitor was interesting.

sentences.en: Every escape in this series produces that sequence.
sentences.zh: 这个系列中的每一次逃脱都会产生这个序列。

sentences.en: None of them required us to know in advance that fsmonitor was interesting.
sentences.zh: 它们都不需要我们提前知道 fsmonitor 很有趣。

blocks.html_en: What A Good Threat Model For Agents Looks Like

sentences.en: What A Good Threat Model For Agents Looks Like
sentences.zh: 一个好的代理威胁模型是什么样的

blocks.html_en: Agentic endpoints require a layered threat model. The relevant controls belong across all levels of an organization's defense: in vendor products, enterprise policy, and the endpoint security layers around the tools: Start from deny-by-default wherever sandboxing is used and then:

sentences.en: Agentic endpoints require a layered threat model.
sentences.zh: 代理端点需要分层威胁模型。

sentences.en: The relevant controls belong across all levels of an organization's defense: in vendor products, enterprise policy, and the endpoint security layers around the tools: Start from deny-by-default wherever sandboxing is used and then:
sentences.zh: 相关控制属于组织防御的所有级别：在供应商产品、企业策略和工具周围的端点安全层中：从使用沙箱的地方默认拒绝开始，然后：

blocks.html_en: • Treat workspace configuration that can trigger execution as sensitive

sentences.en: Treat workspace configuration that can trigger execution as sensitive
sentences.zh: 将可以触发执行的工作区配置视为敏感

blocks.html_en: • Require explicit approval when an agent creates or modifies host-side automation

sentences.en: Require explicit approval when an agent creates or modifies host-side automation
sentences.zh: 当代理创建或修改主机端自动化时需要明确批准

blocks.html_en: • Run helper execution under the same policy as direct agent execution where possible

sentences.en: Run helper execution under the same policy as direct agent execution where possible
sentences.zh: 尽可能在与直接代理执行相同的策略下运行帮助程序执行

blocks.html_en: • Constrain automatic discovery that executes workspace-controlled binaries

sentences.en: Constrain automatic discovery that executes workspace-controlled binaries
sentences.zh: 限制执行工作区控制的二进制文件的自动发现

blocks.html_en: • Model command policy at the invocation and side-effect level

sentences.en: Model command policy at the invocation and side-effect level
sentences.zh: 在调用和副作用级别对命令策略进行建模

blocks.html_en: • Restrict access to privileged local daemons unless explicitly needed

sentences.en: Restrict access to privileged local daemons unless explicitly needed
sentences.zh: 除非明确需要，否则限制对特权本地守护程序的访问

blocks.html_en: • Preserve provenance between user-created, repo-created, and agent-created files

sentences.en: Preserve provenance between user-created, repo-created, and agent-created files
sentences.zh: 保留用户创建的、存储库创建的和代理创建的文件之间的来源

blocks.html_en: • Monitor trust handoffs, not just the agent process

sentences.en: Monitor trust handoffs, not just the agent process
sentences.zh: 监控信任交接，而不仅仅是代理流程

blocks.html_en: It’s essential to understand that agentic development is a new endpoint behavior, not just a nicer or newer IDE.

sentences.en: It’s essential to understand that agentic development is a new endpoint behavior, not just a nicer or newer IDE.
sentences.zh: 必须了解代理开发是一种新的端点行为，而不仅仅是更好或更新的 IDE。

blocks.html_en: Our Week of Sandbox Escapes is a story about a new kind of software breaking an existing assumption - sandboxing your agent is not enough. We need to better understand the threat models of non deterministic, agentic software, and secure them properly.

sentences.en: Our Week of Sandbox Escapes is a story about a new kind of software breaking an existing assumption - sandboxing your agent is not enough.
sentences.zh: 我们的沙箱逃亡周讲述了一种新型软件打破现有假设的故事 - 将代理放在沙箱中是不够的。

sentences.en: We need to better understand the threat models of non deterministic, agentic software, and secure them properly.
sentences.zh: 我们需要更好地理解非确定性、代理软件的威胁模型，并正确保护它们。

blocks.html_en: Developer endpoints were already complicated. Agents add actors that read untrusted content, write plausible-looking files, and trigger automation inside the same environment that holds code, credentials, infrastructure access, and release paths.

sentences.en: Developer endpoints were already complicated.
sentences.zh: 开发人员端点已经很复杂。

sentences.en: Agents add actors that read untrusted content, write plausible-looking files, and trigger automation inside the same environment that holds code, credentials, infrastructure access, and release paths.
sentences.zh: 代理添加参与者来读取不受信任的内容，编写看似合理的文件，并在保存代码、凭证、基础设施访问和发布路径的同一环境中触发自动化。

blocks.html_en: This research highlights that when it comes to agents, the sandbox boundary that developers expect in coding tools -- one that keeps the agent inside the sandbox and the user outside -- breaks down. The boundary we kept finding was both messier and porous, because If an agent gets to write the future inputs of systems, it was never sandboxed in the first place.

sentences.en: This research highlights that when it comes to agents, the sandbox boundary that developers expect in coding tools -- one that keeps the agent inside the sandbox and the user outside -- breaks down.
sentences.zh: 这项研究强调，当涉及到代理时，开发人员在编码工具中期望的沙箱边界（将代理保留在沙箱内而用户保留在沙箱外）被打破。

sentences.en: The boundary we kept finding was both messier and porous, because If an agent gets to write the future inputs of systems, it was never sandboxed in the first place.
sentences.zh: 我们不断发现的边界既混乱又漏洞百出，因为如果一个代理开始编写系统的未来输入，它首先就不会被沙箱化。

blocks.html_en: This is why agentic security requires its own threat model.

sentences.en: This is why agentic security requires its own threat model.
sentences.zh: 这就是代理安全需要自己的威胁模型的原因。

blocks.html_en: Combine the non-deterministic nature of agents with the complexity of modern IT environments and there will be thousands more to come. The industry is learning as we go, which is why it's so important to share what we learn and help enterprises recognize and mitigate the risks when moving quickly to deploy agentic systems.

sentences.en: Combine the non-deterministic nature of agents with the complexity of modern IT environments and there will be thousands more to come.
sentences.zh: 将代理的不确定性与现代 IT 环境的复杂性结合起来，未来还会有数千个代理。

sentences.en: The industry is learning as we go, which is why it's so important to share what we learn and help enterprises recognize and mitigate the risks when moving quickly to deploy agentic systems.
sentences.zh: 该行业正在不断学习，这就是为什么分享我们所学到的知识并帮助企业在快速部署代理系统时识别和减轻风险如此重要。

content_html_zh:
为什么代理安全需要自己的威胁模型

执行摘要

在几个月的时间里，Pillar Research 发现并重现了 Cursor、Codex、Gemini CLI 和 Antigravity 的沙箱逃逸和边界绕过。 几乎在所有情况下，代理都不需要直接破坏沙箱。 它只需要编写一些沙箱外的可信组件稍后可以运行、加载、扫描或视为安全的内容。 总的来说，这些漏洞表明人工智能编码代理改变了端点威胁模型，而大多数沙箱设计还没有跟上。

我们将这项研究发布为“沙盒逃亡周”：每天进行一次深入研究，每次都显示穿越边界的不同路线。 研究结果分为四种可重复的故障模式：

  • 黑名单沙箱无法跟上操作系统复杂性的步伐
  • 工作区配置是真正可执行的代码
  • “安全”命令白名单信任命令名称而不是调用
  • 完全位于沙箱之外的特权本地守护进程

最重要的是，代理的爆炸半径不是代理进程；而是代理的范围。它包括代理可以写入主机稍后信任的所有内容。

代理的生产力提升是真实的，开发人员已经在使用它们。 问题在于，在大多数组织将这些工具作为基础设施进行管理之前，这些工具就已经成为基础设施。

CISO 和安全买家需要认识到，代理 IDE 或 CLI 仅有沙箱是不够的。 它需要证明沙箱边界实际在哪里，代理可以写入什么，哪些主机组件信任这些写入，它可以到达哪些本地守护进程，哪些命令跳过批准，以及当受信任的帮助程序执行代理影响的某些内容时存在哪些遥测。

代理工具正在成为端点参与者。 他们需要像端点参与者一样接受评估、监控和管理。

‍

支柱的方法

在 Pillar，我们从企业风险的角度来看待人工智能的采用。

代理编码工具是一个自然的起点，因为它们将端点风险集中在一个工作流程中。 它们在源代码、SSH 密钥、云令牌、浏览器会话、包发布权限和生产访问通常紧密结合在一起的地方运行。 他们按照惯例处理不受信任的输入：自述文件、问题、文档、依赖项、代码注释、差异、日志和 Web 内容。 它们是为行动而生，而不仅仅是回答。

把它们放在一起，即时注入就不再是聊天机器人的问题了。 恶意指令可能成为开发人员计算机上的本地操作。

大多数产品通过沙箱、批准提示和安全命令策略来解决该风险，但这些控制通常只关注代理流程。 代理的功能还包括它可以写入的文件以及稍后信任这些文件的主机组件。

特工如何逃离沙箱

沙箱的一般经验法则是允许在工作区内部使用，而在工作区外部则受到保护。

实际上，沙箱边界至少有三层：

  • 直接执行：代理进程可以运行什么。
  • 工作区写入：代理可以创建或更改哪些文件。
  • 主机信任：未沙盒组件稍后对这些文件执行的操作。

第三层是最有趣的失败所在。 现代 IDE 和 CLI 充满了主机端自动化：

  • Python 扩展发现解释器
  • Git 集成扫描存储库
  • VSCode 任务运行程序加载项目任务。
  • Hook 引擎触发生命周期命令
  • Docker Desktop 公开了强大的本地套接字

沙盒代理可以遵循给定的每条规则，并且仍然可以调整这些组件消耗的输入。

这就是想象的或感知的边界：相信“代理只能在项目内部写入”与“代理不能影响主机”相同。

但这是一个错误的假设。 在开发人员端点上，项目文件通常是可执行的基础设施。

该系列

每篇文章都深入一条链。 它们共同展示了跨供应商和产品重复出现的相同故障模式。

失败模式 1：拒绝者输给了平台复杂性

反重力安全带的发现是拒绝名单失败的最明显的例子。 从“默认允许”开始的沙箱配置文件必须记住操作系统公开的每个危险操作：每个本地服务、安装类型、启动路径以及它们之间的奇怪交互。

这不是一个沙箱，而是一个有人记得要阻止的事物列表，而且总是缺少一个条目。

代理使情况变得更糟，因为攻击者在环境中获得了灵活的操作员。 该模型可以适应、写入文件、运行命令、重试以及以静态策略未预料到的方式组合功能。

失败模式 2：工作区配置通常是代码

有几个发现并不是典型的流程突破。 代理写入了允许写入的文件。 当主机将这些文件视为可信配置时，逃逸发生了。

我们通过“.vscode 中的定时炸弹：绕过 Antigravity 的安全模式”以及“钩子已经在工作区中”漏洞、virtualenv 解释器、Git 配置和 fsmonitor 帮助程序看到了这一点。

需要注意的是，这些都是正常的开发人员工作流程。 多年来，项目配置告诉机器如何构建、测试、启动、lint 和自动化存储库。 当沙盒编写器可以将可执行配置交给未沙盒读取器时，边界上就有一个洞。

代理打破了旧的假设，即这些配置是由人类编写并以通常的方式进行审查的。 开发人员越早适应这一现实越好。

失败模式 3：“安全”命令实际上并不安全

Codex git show 发现被允许，因为该名称看起来是只读的。 实际的调用并非如此。

Git 充满了改变行为的标志。 命令看起来无害，但仍然可以写入文件、加载配置、调用帮助程序、运行挂钩或触发用户定义的行为。 在那个世界里，通过命令名称列入白名单太生硬了。

政策问题不是“git show 安全吗？” 它是：正在运行什么确切的调用，使用什么参数，在什么目录中，针对什么配置，以及可能产生哪些副作用？

故障模式 4：本地守护进程在盒子外运行

Docker 套接字的发现提醒我们，对代理进程进行沙箱处理并不会对主机进行沙箱处理。

特权本地守护进程是第二个执行环境。 如果代理可以与其对话，则守护进程可以执行代理本身不允许执行的工作。 开发人员端点通常运行 Docker Desktop、包管理器、云 CLI、语言服务器、构建守护程序、模拟器和本地数据库。 其中任何一个都可以成为一座信任的桥梁。

阻止直接文件系统写入但使主机到达的守护程序保持打开状态的沙箱并未保护主机。 它将边界移至守护程序 API。

安全团队应该向编码供应商询问什么

购买谈话不应停留在“它有沙盒吗？” 对于代理系统，强制性问题包括：

  • 代理可以写什么？
  • 哪些主机组件信任这些写入？
  • 代理可以访问哪些本地守护进程？
  • 哪些命令会跳过批准，为什么？
  • 策略是否针对命令名称、路径或实际副作用强制执行？
  • 产品能否区分用户创建的项目状态和代理创建的项目状态？
  • 当受信任的助手运行代理编写的内容时，会触发什么遥测？

这些问题将营销平台中列为复选框功能的沙箱与为保护主机上的代理进程提供可执行边界的沙箱区分开来。

支柱的方法

我们的方法从代理威胁建模开始。 有些东西向代理发送不受信任的输入，代理生成输出，其他组件使用输出并对其进行操作。 系统中的大多数其他组件都是在代理出现之前构建的。 如果控件仅监视代理进程，则它仅保护该链的一个链接。 因此，我们对任何代理系统都会提出以下问题：

  • 哪些不受信任的输入可以到达模型？
  • 模型可以直接做什么？
  • 代理可以写什么？
  • 哪些组件读取这些写入？
  • 其中哪些执行代理策略之外的代码？
  • 哪些控制是确定性的，哪些取决于模型行为？
  • 代理创建的工件在哪里可以成为主机操作？

当谈到如何对端点进行建模时，我们将其视为一组信任切换，而不是单个进程树。 代理将工作交给在 CI、浏览器、SaaS 工作流程和开发人员计算机上信任他们的系统。

我们通过关注切换没有阻塞点的地方来构建端点解决方案。 在 CI 中，消耗代理输出的构建步骤在您可以记录的地方运行。 在 SaaS 中，切换会跨越您已有的 API。 在开发人员计算机上，代理写入一个文件，一个特权本地进程在几微秒后在同一用户会话中拾取该文件，没有网络跃点，也没有任何东西监视接缝。 默认情况下，切换是不可见的。

这就是我们的端点产品所检测到的：接缝。 不是代理进程，也不是危险文件名列表。 代理所写内容的出处、对哪个未沙盒组件读取它的可见性，以及执行时的记录。

这个系列中的每一次逃脱都会产生这个序列。 它们都不需要我们提前知道 fsmonitor 很有趣。

一个好的代理威胁模型是什么样的

代理端点需要分层威胁模型。 相关控制属于组织防御的所有级别：在供应商产品、企业策略和工具周围的端点安全层中：从使用沙箱的地方默认拒绝开始，然后：

  • 将可以触发执行的工作区配置视为敏感
  • 当代理创建或修改主机端自动化时需要明确批准
  • 尽可能在与直接代理执行相同的策略下运行帮助程序执行
  • 限制执行工作区控制的二进制文件的自动发现
  • 在调用和副作用级别对命令策略进行建模
  • 除非明确需要，否则限制对特权本地守护程序的访问
  • 保留用户创建的、存储库创建的和代理创建的文件之间的来源
  • 监控信任交接，而不仅仅是代理流程

必须了解代理开发是一种新的端点行为，而不仅仅是更好或更新的 IDE。

我们的沙箱逃亡周讲述了一种新型软件打破现有假设的故事 - 将代理放在沙箱中是不够的。 我们需要更好地理解非确定性、代理软件的威胁模型，并正确保护它们。

开发人员端点已经很复杂。 代理添加参与者来读取不受信任的内容，编写看似合理的文件，并在保存代码、凭证、基础设施访问和发布路径的同一环境中触发自动化。

这项研究强调，当涉及到代理时，开发人员在编码工具中期望的沙箱边界（将代理保留在沙箱内而用户保留在沙箱外）被打破。 我们不断发现的边界既混乱又漏洞百出，因为如果一个代理开始编写系统的未来输入，它首先就不会被沙箱化。

这就是代理安全需要自己的威胁模型的原因。

将代理的不确定性与现代 IT 环境的复杂性结合起来，未来还会有数千个代理。 该行业正在不断学习，这就是为什么分享我们所学到的知识并帮助企业在快速部署代理系统时识别和减轻风险如此重要。
